hints.Danaos: 'Danaos' is a noun — the direct object (the Greeks)
hints.ferentes: 'ferentes' is a present participle, not the main verb

hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'timebam' — 'Timeo' is present 1st person singular
hints.Perfect: Perfect would be 'timui' — 'Timeo' uses the present stem
hints.Future: Future would be 'timebo' — 'Timeo' is present indicative

hints.teneo, tenere, tenui, tentum: These are the principal parts of 'tenere' (to hold)
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the principal parts of 'videre' (to see)
hints.habeo, habere, habui, habitum: These are the principal parts of 'habere' (to have)

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'Danai' — 'Danaos' with -os ending is accusative plural
'dona' could be nominative neuter plural, but here it's the object of 'ferentes'

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'donum' with -um ending is 2nd declension neuter
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied endings — '-um' is characteristic of 2nd declension neuter
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'donum' is 2nd declension neuter

hints.Perfect passive participle: Perfect passive participle of 'ferre' would be 'lati' — 'ferentes' has the -nt- present participle stem
hints.Gerundive: Gerundive would be 'ferendi' — 'ferentes' has the -nt- present participle marker

hints.Nominative: 'ferentes' could be nominative plural, but it modifies 'Danaos' which is accusative
hints.Genitive: Genitive plural of the participle would be 'ferentium'
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural of the participle would be 'ferentibus'

translation: I fear the Greeks even bearing gifts
